verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation/palmitoylation. Its catalytic body faces the cytosol and is not exposed extracellularly. Checking contextual buckets: no documented cancer/cell-death/activation-induced outer-leaflet display; no narrow germline/developmental ecto-pool (broadly expressed in hematopoietic lineages on the inner leaflet); not a lysosomal TM protein subject to lysosomal exocytosis; no documented minority outer-leaflet PM pool or cycling; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering exposing the kinase ectofacially. Clinical small-molecule SFK inhibitors engage the intracellular kinase domain.
